_metadata.description: Controlled vocabulary for the 6 qualitative evidence-strength tiers used by ingredient_interaction_rules evidence_level fields. This is intentionally separate from evidence_level_vocab.json, which covers study-design tiers in backed_clinical_studies.
_metadata.purpose: Single source of truth for interaction-rule evidence strength labels and descriptions. Source data still uses the field name evidence_level; this vocab names the concept evidence_strength to avoid conflating rule strength with clinical-study design.
_metadata.alignment: Pipeline source: scripts/data/ingredient_interaction_rules.json and scripts/data/ingredient_interaction_rules_Reviewed.json evidence_level fields. Flutter consumer: future replacement for lib/core/constants/severity.dart EvidenceLevel enum.

field_contract.id: Stable snake_case identifier found in interaction-rule evidence_level fields.
field_contract.name: User-facing label.
field_contract.notes: ≤200-char plain-English description shown in tap-to-learn modal.

evidence_strengths.name: Established evidence
evidence_strengths.notes: Well-supported rule with consistent clinical or regulatory backing. Treat as the strongest interaction evidence tier.

evidence_strengths.name: Probable evidence
evidence_strengths.notes: Likely clinically relevant based on credible human, pharmacology, or regulatory evidence, but not as definitive as established.

evidence_strengths.name: Moderate evidence
evidence_strengths.notes: Meaningful but mixed or narrower evidence. Use for rule context where support is stronger than limited but not probable.

evidence_strengths.name: Limited evidence
evidence_strengths.notes: Sparse or early evidence. Useful for context, but should not be presented as a firm clinical conclusion.

evidence_strengths.name: Theoretical evidence
evidence_strengths.notes: Mechanism-based concern without direct confirmation. Present as possible risk, not as a proven interaction.

evidence_strengths.name: No evidence data
evidence_strengths.notes: No usable evidence tier is available for this rule. Keep copy neutral and avoid implying a proven risk.
